text: Here we see striking from the hand being performed by an elite player.
Note the position of the head, hands, hurley and feet.
Now let's look at each of the key teaching points for striking from the
Hold the ball in the non-dominant hand with the elbow slightly bent.
The shoulders and feet should be in line with the target.
Toss the ball from the cupped hand to shoulder height. Keep eyes on the ball.
Slide the non-dominant hand into the lock position, bending the elbows to raise the hurley.
The hurley should be in the same position as the head.
The head should be upright in front of the dominant shoulder with the boss above head height.
Step forward with the lead foot and swing the hurley downward with a wristy action.
Strike the ball between knee and hip level.
Transfer the body weight from the dominant leg to the non-dominant leg.
When performing the strike from the hand on the non-dominant side, raise the hurley to the non-dominant shoulder.
And transfer body weight from the non-dominant leg to the dominant leg as the swing is completed.
